pattern: Build a mask of all ones the same width as the number's binary form, then XOR with that mask to flip exactly the significant bits while leaving the (nonexistent) leading zeros untouched.
hints: Flipping every bit of the machine word would turn the implicit leading zeros into ones too, which is wrong. You only want to flip the bits up to and including the highest set bit.
Find the bit length L of num (the position of its highest set bit). A mask of L ones is (1 << L) - 1.
Return num XOR mask, where mask = (1 << num.bit_length()) - 1, so only the significant bits are inverted.

edge_inputs.description: 1 is a single set bit, complement is 0
all significant bits already one, complement is 0
power of two has a long run of trailing zeros to flip
largest value within the constraint range, all 31 bits set
value just below a power of two, all bits set

stress.description: random values across the allowed range
stress.note: covers small numbers, powers of two, and near-ceiling values
